summary: Partially decompiled the fighter demo creation/archive helpers into a new `src/melee/ft/ftdemo.c` with a matching report of 99.18% for the file. The PR moved several functions formerly living in `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` into named C APIs such as `ftDemo_CreateFighter`, `ftDemo_ObjAllocInit`, `ftDemo_SetArchiveData`, `ftDemo_GetMotionFileString`, and `ftDemo_SetFacingDirection`, while leaving some remaining surrounding functions in asm and exposing their labels through `ftdemo.h`. It also added build wiring, forward declarations, type/header cleanup, and call-site renames in player and game-mode assembly to use the new demo APIs.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/ftdemo.c` and `src/melee/ft/ftdemo.h` for demo-related fighter setup functions previously represented as anonymous or `ftCo_` symbols in `ft_08A4.s`.

Decompiled `ftDemo_CreateFighter`, including GObj creation, GX link setup, fighter allocation, userdata initialization, action-state list setup, costume/effect loading, parts/animation/collision initialization, shadow/camera setup, proc registration, optional demo callback dispatch, and final `ftLib_800867E8` call.

Added smaller demo helpers: `ftDemo_ObjAllocInit`, `ftDemo_SetArchiveData`, `ftDemo_GetMotionFileString`, and `ftDemo_SetFacingDirection`; the PR report shows the latter three matched at 99.76%, 100%, and 100% respectively, and `ftDemo_ObjAllocInit` at 100%.

Kept `ftDemo_CreateFighter` as an `ASM` function with an inline asm fallback guarded by `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)`, reflecting that it was close but not fully matched; the report lists it at 98.85%.

Removed a large decompiled range from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`, while retaining and globalizing remaining labels such as `ftCo_800BECB0`, `ftCo_800BED88`, `ftCo_800BEF04`, `ftCo_800BEFD4`, and `ftCo_800BF034` so C code could reference them.
Added `asm/melee/ft/ft_3C61.s` and `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureKirby.s` to preserve remaining assembly/data, including `.sdata` assertion strings like `jobj.h`, `jobj`, and `scale`.
Updated `obj_files.mk` to compile `src/melee/ft/ftdemo.c.o` plus the newly split asm objects.

Renamed call sites in `src/melee/pl/player.c`: `ftCo_800BE7E0` became `ftDemo_CreateFighter`, `ftCo_800BEB60` became `ftDemo_SetArchiveData`, and `ftCo_SetFacingDirection` became `ftDemo_SetFacingDirection`.
Changed several game-mode and unknown assembly call sites to call `ftDemo_ObjAllocInit` before `Player_InitAllPlayers`.
Moved `plAllocInfo2` out of `player.c` into player type/forward headers so `ftdemo.c` could accept a typed `plAllocInfo2*` parameter.

Exposed or refined fighter data declarations needed by the demo code, including `ftData_UnkDemoCallbacks0`, `ftData_UnkIntPairs`, `ftData_803C2468`, `ftData_803C24EC`, `ftData_803C52A0`, `ftData_UnkMotionStates0`, and `ftData_UnkBytePerCharacter`.
Refined the `_ftData` layout around offsets `+0x14`, `+0x18`, and `+0x1C`, and moved `ftData_UnkCountStruct` from `ftdata.c` into `src/melee/ft/types.h`.
Added `src/melee/ft/ft_0C88.h` for `ftCo_800C884C`, reducing dependence on broader `ft_08A4.h` declarations.

Cleaned baselib archive forward declarations by converting archive-related typedef bodies to named `struct HSD_Archive*` style declarations and adding forward typedefs in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/forward.h`.
Removed the generic `INVOKE` macro from `src/Runtime/platform.h`; the new code uses explicit null checks before callback invocation.
smart_moves: Gave semantic names to demo-specific APIs instead of keeping them as `ftCo_800BE7E0`, `ftCo_800BEB60`, and `ftCo_SetFacingDirection`, making player demo setup call sites searchable and easier to reason about.
Used a dedicated `ftdemo.h` to collect the new demo APIs and the still-assembly callbacks used by `on_create_fighter`, avoiding pollution of the broad `ft_08A4.h` header.
Split out a small `ft_0C88.h` for `ftCo_800C884C`, which let the new C file include a precise dependency rather than relying on an oversized common header.
Preserved matching safety for the difficult 840-byte `ftDemo_CreateFighter` by keeping inline asm fallback under matching guards while still landing the readable C version.
Converted anonymous or local assembly labels to global labels where the C translation needed function-pointer references, especially for the `on_create_fighter[16]` table.
Typed `HSD_Archive*`, `plAllocInfo2*`, and `ftData_UnkCountStruct` dependencies rather than using raw integers or local duplicate structs, which improved header reuse for later decompilation.
decomp_lessons: Large initialization functions can be partially landed by isolating the nearest semantic subsystem file, keeping an inline asm fallback for the imperfect function, and matching smaller helpers first.
When decompiling code that uses callback tables, expose the still-assembly targets as global symbols and declare them in the new subsystem header so C tables can reference them directly.
Replacing numeric `ftCo_` symbols with subsystem-prefixed names such as `ftDemo_*` makes call-site intent clearer and helps subsequent PRs find related code.

If a function depends on a local duplicate struct in another C file, promote the struct to shared `types.h`/`forward.h` headers before moving the function; this PR did that for `plAllocInfo2` and `ftData_UnkCountStruct`.
Archive access code benefits from using typed `HSD_Archive*` signatures instead of integer placeholders, because `HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress` call sites then become self-documenting.
Do not over-declare functions in broad headers after extracting a subsystem; prune old declarations from `ft_08A4.h`/dox and put the extracted APIs in the new subsystem header.
naming_conventions: `ftDemo_` prefix was introduced for fighter demo lifecycle/archive/facing helpers: `ftDemo_CreateFighter`, `ftDemo_ObjAllocInit`, `ftDemo_SetArchiveData`, `ftDemo_GetMotionFileString`, and `ftDemo_SetFacingDirection`.
Remaining unknown common-fighter callbacks retained address-style `ftCo_800Bxxxx` names but were declared with typed `Fighter_GObj* gobj` parameters in `ftdemo.h`.
Callback/function pointer parameter names were normalized away from `this` toward `gobj`, as seen in `Fighter_ItemEvent` and `Fighter_UnkMtxEvent` typedefs.
Forward headers use explicit typedefs such as `typedef struct plAllocInfo2 plAllocInfo2;` and `typedef struct HSD_Archive HSD_Archive;` to avoid needing full definitions at every include site.
Address comments in headers were preserved for newly named functions, e.g. `/* 0BE7E0 */ Fighter_GObj* ftDemo_CreateFighter(...)`, supporting address-based search.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR report shows `src/melee/ft/ftdemo.c` at 99.18% overall, with `ftDemo_CreateFighter` at 98.85%, `ftDemo_SetArchiveData` at 99.76%, and three helper functions at 100%.
Used `ASM` plus `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` to keep a readable C body for `ftDemo_CreateFighter` while retaining exact inline assembly under the alternative branch.
Moved matched or nearly matched code out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`, but left unmatched ranges in asm and made needed labels global.

Added separate asm objects for leftover material/data (`asm/melee/ft/ft_3C61.s` and `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureKirby.s`) and wired them before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o` in `obj_files.mk`.
Reconstructed the demo create callback table as `static HSD_GObjEvent on_create_fighter[16]`, including a deliberate `NULL` slot and references to both decompiled and still-assembly handlers.
Manually expanded callback invocation with explicit null checks instead of using the removed generic `INVOKE` macro, matching the control flow visible in assembly.
Recreated archive lookup logic with a small static integer table `{ 9, 10, 11, 14, 15 }` and `arr_idx >= 4` branch to select between `ftDemo_GetMotionFileString` and direct demo string table entries.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `ftDemo_CreateFighter` or remove its inline asm fallback.
Search for subsequent renames of `ftCo_800BECB0`, `ftCo_800BED88`, `ftCo_800BEF04`, `ftCo_800BEFD4`, and other demo callback table entries.
Look for PRs that refine `plAllocInfo2` and determine whether it was unified with `struct plAllocInfo` as the old player.c TODO suggested.
Search later changes to `ftData_UnkDemoCallbacks0`, `ftData_803C2468`, and `ftData_803C24EC` for better names and stronger types.
Check whether `ftData_UnkCountStruct` fields `data` and `count` were later renamed based on archive/demo motion semantics.
Find follow-up header cleanup around `HSD_Archive` and baselib forward declarations.
